source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Standing behavioral test-coverage gate in verify.py (C2)
what_failed: The suite is ~45% plumbing; nothing enforces that a behavioral module has a real behavioral test - how T-148 shipped green. T-159 lists 7 such modules.
why_likely: Tests were added ad hoc; no structural rule ties source modules to behavioral tests.
migration_plan: 1. Add a verify.py check mapping source modules (agent/, tools/, core/) -> testing/test_<name>.py.
2. Start as WARN (report modules lacking a test) to avoid a wall of red; flip to FAIL once T-159 is cleared.
3. Keep a small explicit allowlist for genuinely non-behavioral modules.
4. Reuse review_guard.py's test-coverage heuristic; do not duplicate it.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: a name-map check + allowlist, WARN before FAIL. The standing contract that makes 'no behavioral module without a real test' real.
analysis: C2: the detector that should have caught C1. Pair the gate with the backlog (T-159) so green means something.
